channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Nigeria's import duty waivers reached ₦34 trillion as Customs highlights security and economic support measures
item.description: The Comptroller-General of the Nigeria Customs Service (NCS), Bashir Adeniyi, told the Senate Committee on Finance that import duty waivers granted through Import Duty Exemption Certificates (IDECs) r...

item.title: Adeyemi Faces Charges Over PFIPC Scandal as US Firm Offers Asylum Support
item.description: The embattled Director-General of the controversial Presidential Foreign Intervention Promotion Council (PFIPC), Adeyemi Adeniyi, has declared readiness to face charges related to the scandal involvin...

item.title: Health Canada report highlights regulatory failures at Grifols plasma donation clinics
item.description: A Health Canada inspection report has revealed significant regulatory lapses at the private plasma collection company Grifols, including repeated clearance of ineligible donors at risk for Creutzfeldt...

item.title: UK Heatwave Continues with Prolonged Dry Spells and Wildfire Risks
item.description: The UK is experiencing an extended period of dry weather, with no significant rainfall expected for at least a week. This has led to record-breaking temperatures, hosepipe bans affecting eight million...

item.title: Trump Dismisses Election Commission Members Amid Midterm Election Concerns

item.title: Trump's Sons Benefit from Father's Policies, Report Reveals
item.description: A Washington Post investigation reveals that Donald Trump Jr. and Eric Trump have profited significantly from their father's policies through investments in defense-related ventures. The report states...

item.title: Marikina Police Investigation Leads to Arrest of 5 Suspects in 'Gapos Gang' Case
item.description: The Philippine National Police (PNP) Eastern Police District conducted a successful operation against the 'Gapos Gang,' a criminal group that impersonated law enforcement officers in Marikina City. Th...

item.title: Vintage Photos Reveal 1950s Massachusetts Weddings' Elegance and Tradition
item.description: This article presents a curated collection of 50 vibrant color photographs capturing weddings in Massachusetts during the late 1950s, offering a nostalgic glimpse into mid-century American wedding cus...

item.title: Gym noise disrupts hospital operations near Perth
item.description: A Perth hospital director has described the noise from a nearby gym as 'like bombs being dropped', claiming vibrations from music and equipment could force cancellations of surgeries. The dispute invo...

item.title: Lawyer Levi Baligod Faces Arrest Warrant in Cyber Libel Case
item.description: Levi Baligod, a lawyer, has been issued an arrest warrant by the Makati City Regional Trial Court, Branch 148, in connection with a cyber libel case filed by Agriculture Secretary Francisco Tiu Laurel...

item.title: Christopher Nolan addresses backlash over 'The Odyssey' casting choices
item.description: Christopher Nolan has addressed the backlash surrounding casting choices in his new film 'The Odyssey', calling the criticism 'irrelevant'. The film, an adaptation of Homer's ancient Greek epic, featu...

item.title: KZN Athletics President Apologizes for Street Brawl Incident
item.description: KwaZulu-Natal Athletics President Steve Mkasi has issued a public apology on his Facebook page following a viral video of a street altercation. The incident, captured on camera, shows Mkasi and an uni...

item.title: Regulatory Challenges Hinder E-Bike Adoption in Pakistan
item.description: The National Assembly Standing Committee on Industries highlighted significant regulatory challenges hindering the electric bike rollout in Pakistan. A fragmented framework across multiple ministries,...

item.title: Contempt case against police over non-compliance with court order
item.description: The article details a contempt case filed against Nigerian police authorities for failing to comply with a court order issued in September 2025. The order, delivered by Justice Mrs. Nyako, directed th...

item.title: Tragic Bangkok bar fire claims 27 lives as investigations begin
item.description: A devastating fire at a music bar in Bangkok, Thailand, has claimed at least 27 lives and left 25 critically injured, marking the deadliest blaze in the city in 17 years. The incident occurred at the ...

item.title: Anti-social groups dump rubbish near childcare centre, sparking calls for stricter laws
item.description: The opposition has criticized the ACT government for its slow response to anti-social behaviour, including illegal rubbish dumping and drug paraphernalia found near a childcare centre. Opposition MLA ...

item.title: Major platforms fail to use tools against sextortion despite new report
item.description: A new report reveals major social media platforms in Australia are not effectively using available tools to combat sexual extortion, despite the crime following a predictable script. Over 3,300 report...

item.title: Philippines’ Global Health Ambitions Continue Despite Herbosa’s Resignation
item.description: The resignation of Health Secretary Teodoro Herbosa marks the end of a significant chapter for Philippine healthcare, but it does not signal the conclusion of the country's global health aspirations. ...

item.title: Political polarization and the emotional divide in public discourse
item.description: The article examines the contrasting reactions to the death of Ann Widdecombe, a prominent political figure, highlighting the asymmetrical emotional responses from different political factions. Daniel...
